title: attribution — retention windows
description: Attribution profiles are retained forever while their subject is active. Touchpoints retain 730 days default (2 years) — extended to 2555 days (7 years) on Enterprise via attribution_touchpoint_retention_extended. Reset attributions purge touchpoints 90 days post-request (audit hold). Anonymous session profiles archive at 730 days from last_touch_at. Aligns with GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) storage-limitation principle — shortest legitimate retention consistent with legitimate interest.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) — storage limitation (base retention window)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with 90-day audit hold on reset)
gdpr Art. 25 — data protection by design (default 730d; extended retention is opt-in)
ccpa/cpra Section 1798.100 + 1798.105 — right to know + right to delete
soc2 CC6.3 — audit trail retention (audit rows outlive source rows)

purge.trigger: user erasure (RedactAttributionOnUserErasure) OR tenant erasure (FK CASCADE)
purge.notes: Active profiles are the tenant's canonical marketing-attribution state. Only erasure paths (user or tenant) hard-purge them.

hot.description: Retained 2 years post-churn for potential re-engagement

purge.notes: The soft-delete releases the (tenant_id, subject_type, subject_id) unique slot so a fresh attribution can start if the subject re-engages years later.
The 90-day audit hold matches the AttributionResetRequested → AttributionResetConfirmed flow. Post-hold hard-delete fires AttributionResetConfirmed to close the erasure loop.

hot.description: Base retention window. Applies to Small + Medium tiers. Non-Enterprise tenants only.

purge.trigger: > 730 days past captured_at + tenant lacks attribution_touchpoint_retention_extended entitlement
purge.notes: The 2-year window aligns with the default analytics retention downstream. Touchpoints past 730 days rarely contribute to actionable analysis.

hot.description: Enterprise-only. Extended retention for financial-audit alignment (7 years matches finance module invoice/subscription retention).

purge.trigger: > 2555 days past captured_at + tenant holds attribution_touchpoint_retention_extended entitlement
attribution has an AttributionResetRequested audit row > 90 days old
purge.action: hard delete every associated touchpoint + fire AttributionResetConfirmed
purge.notes: The 90-day audit hold complies with GDPR Art. 17 without violating the operational need for compliance-team review of erasure requests.

retention.notes: first_touch_* fields are IMMUTABLE post-create. They are preserved for the life of the profile per attribution.retention.first_touch_forever config. The audit trail row for AttributionFirstTouchRecorded retains 7 years regardless of the source-row retention.

last_touch_* fields overwrite on every attribution-bearing request. Historical last_touch values live in the AttributionLastTouchUpdated audit trail with 90-day retention (frequent-mutation event; longer retention is unnecessary for compliance).

Consent snapshot on the attribution row is the CURRENT state. Per-touchpoint consent snapshots (on each attribution_touchpoints row) are the ORIGINAL state at capture-time — those are the regulator-queryable proof-of-consent records.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention. That includes AttributionResetRequested + AttributionResetConfirmed + AttributionMerged + AttributionConverted events.
TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every attribution row + touchpoint (FK cascade), but the audit rows written during those events survive the cascade (per audit module's outlives-source design).

UserErased (individual erasure) redacts fields but does NOT hard-delete — the attribution row survives for aggregate metrics with subject_id=null. The 90-day hard-delete happens via the reset audit hold if the user also requested reset (GDPR is opt-in about erasure; deletion at UserErasure alone is not automatic).
